mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and residential fixed investment indicates robust consumer confidence and credit availability, driving sustained equity outperformance over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase expands corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings catch up to capacity utilization.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates negatively with forward equity returns during recovery periods, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show zero correlation with 60-day forward equity returns across multiple decades, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index breaches a high inflation threshold, real yields spike and compress equity valuations, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when CPI exceeds the threshold, or the forward return distribution shows no statistical difference from non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market, reducing recession risk and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts signals a credit crunch and weakening aggregate demand, triggering a sustained negative drift in S&P 500 prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not significantly negative following the housing decline threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for interest rate changes.
